title: Content Filtering for a GenAI App
description:
## Keep the Model On-Policy

A company is launching a customer-facing assistant built on **Amazon Bedrock**. Compliance requires that the assistant **refuses a defined set of off-limits topics**, **blocks harmful/toxic content** in both user prompts and model responses, and **redacts personally identifiable information (PII)** — and the control must keep working even if the team swaps the underlying foundation model later.

What is the most appropriate AWS-native control?

options.text: Configure Amazon Bedrock Guardrails with denied topics, content filters, and PII redaction, and attach it to the model invocations
Put AWS WAF in front of the application to block disallowed words in HTTP requests
Run Amazon Macie on the conversation logs after the fact to find sensitive data
Fine-tune the foundation model so it never produces disallowed content

explanation: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails is purpose-built for exactly this: you define denied topics, content filters (hate, violence, etc.), and PII handling (block or redact), and the policy is evaluated on both the prompt and the response — independently of which foundation model is used, so it survives a model swap. AWS WAF (b) inspects HTTP traffic, not model semantics. Macie (c) is detective and after-the-fact, not a real-time preventive guardrail. Fine-tuning (d) is model-specific, expensive, brittle, and would have to be redone for every model change.

hints.title: Decouple policy from the model
hints.body: The requirement to keep working across model changes points to a control layered on top of the model, not baked into it.
